findings.title: Blog product archive pages missing explicit breadcrumb navigation
findings.description: Product archive pages like /blog/product/tnbm-tarot have BreadcrumbList schema but lack visible breadcrumb UI. Users see only a back-link to '/blog', losing clear path context. Schema helps search engines but visible breadcrumbs improve UX and crawl priority signals.
findings.evidence: Back-link only: ← All posts. BreadcrumbList defined in schema but no matching UI component.
findings.impact: Search engines and users benefit from explicit breadcrumb trails. Missing UI reduces navigation clarity and slightly impacts crawl priority signals.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add breadcrumb UI component:
  1. Home
  2. Blog
  3. {product.blogName}

findings.title: Missing FAQ schema on blog articles with implicit Q&A structure
findings.description: FAQPage schema is only included when frontmatter has 'faqs' array. Articles with question-style headings and answer paragraphs lack FAQ markup. AI search engines and voice assistants miss Q&A content patterns and can't extract answers for indexing.
findings.evidence: FAQPage schema conditionally applied only when post.data.faqs exists. Articles with implicit Q&A patterns (e.g., 'tnbm-reflective-ai.md') don't trigger schema.
findings.impact: Voice assistants and AI search engines (Alexa, Google Assistant, Bing Chat) rely on FAQPage schema. Without it, this content is missed for Q&A indexing and featured snippets.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add explicit 'faqs' to blog frontmatter:
faqs:
  - question: "Is The New Beautiful Me a fortune-telling app?"
    answer: "No. It uses tarot as a framework for self-reflection..."

findings.title: Product blog archive meta descriptions are generic
findings.description: Product archive pages use a template-generated description that's repetitive and lacks keyword variety. Descriptions like 'A journaling app covering... Built for individuals, with a privacy-first product posture' don't optimize for search intent (tarot app, journaling software, self-reflection tools).
findings.evidence: Template: description={`${product.blogName} covering ${product.description.toLowerCase()} Built for ${product.audience}, with a privacy-first product posture.`}
findings.impact: Generic descriptions reduce click-through rates in search results. Unique, keyword-rich descriptions improve CTR and content relevance signals.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add blogDescription field to product data with unique, keyword-optimized descriptions for each product. Example: 'Explore tarot journaling, self-reflection tools, and AI-powered insights. Read articles on tarot apps, reflection practices, and privacy-first design.'

findings.title: Parallax and fade-in animations may impact Core Web Vitals on slower devices
findings.description: Homepage uses parallax scrolling (data-parallax) and staggered fade-in animations requiring JavaScript execution during scroll. This can cause layout shifts and input delays on slower devices, impacting Interaction to Next Paint (INP) and perceived performance.
findings.evidence: Parallax divs: <div class="hero-bg" data-parallax="0.3">. Fade-in: <div class="fade-in fade-in-delay-1">. Both require JS handlers.
findings.impact: Parallax and animation handlers increase main-thread work on scroll, affecting INP on slower devices. Visual appeal is high but performance ranking may be slightly impacted.
findings.fix_suggestion: Optimize by: 1) Respecting prefers-reduced-motion media query, 2) Adding explicit container heights to prevent CLS, 3) Disabling parallax on slower connections using navigator.connection, 4) Deferring parallax handler loading.
